exemplar.title: Analysis on how Angela Carter subverts traditional gender roles in fairy tales through her portrayal of female protagonists.

detailedExemplarData.title: Analysis on how Angela Carter subverts traditional gender roles in fairy tales through her portrayal of female protagonists.

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Confident reference to four separate stories with secure grasp of plot and overarching feminist purpose
Sustained connections to second-wave feminism and fairy-tale conventions
Relevant quotations from each story that generally anchor and advance the argument
Introduction situates Carterâs collection effectively in its historical context
Bibliography credits both primary and secondary materials appropriately
weaknesses: In-text citations omit page numbers, weakening academic rigour
Discussion sometimes remains at a general level and does not probe complex ambiguities
Reliance on paraphrase in places rather than precise quotation detail
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates good knowledge and understanding of Carterâs collection, with sustained interpretation of the feminist implications and relevant textual support. References are generally well chosen and support the argument effectively, though the omission of page numbers and occasional generality prevent an excellent (band 5) rating. Overall, it aligns with band 4 descriptors for this criterion.

strengths: Clear analytical comments on Carterâs inversion of the damsel trope and grotesque portrayal of the Marquis
Insightful reading of the âthrusting pistonsâ metaphor linking mechanical imagery to sexual awakening
Convincing evaluation of the protagonistâs laughter as signifying agency
weaknesses: Frequent lapses into narrative summary rather than close analysis
Overreliance on plot synopsis reduces analytical depth
Evaluative claims often broad and lacking in examination of specific stylistic techniques
Limited unpacking of syntax, tone, and imagery in relation to feminist subversion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis is generally appropriate, with moments of insight, but is too reliant on summary and broad assertions. Evaluation of authorial intention and stylistic features remains limited, fitting the band 3 descriptor of 'generally appropriate analysis' rather than the more consistent insight required for higher bands.

strengths: Logical essay structure with clear introduction, body sections, and conclusion
Paragraphs develop clear ideas that link back to the central inquiry
Headings and section sequencing enhance cohesion
Quotations are mostly well integrated into commentary
weaknesses: Occasional disruptive or duplicate headings interrupt the flow
Conclusion restates rather than synthesizes arguments
Abrupt transitions between story examples without clear topic sentences
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay is well organized and mostly cohesive, with a clear line of inquiry and purposeful integration of examples. Minor structural hiccupsâduplicate headings, abrupt transitions, and a non-synthesizing conclusionâprevent it from achieving the highest level of cohesion and development, supporting a band 4 score.

strengths: Language is generally clear and fluent with varied sentence structures
Good accuracy in grammar, vocabulary, and sentence construction despite some lapses
Academic register is consistently maintained, with appropriate diction
Connective phrases help guide the reader through arguments
weaknesses: Spelling and typographical errors (âindependantâ, stray LaTeX fragment) detract from polish
Repetitive adverbs and passive constructions dilute precision
Occasional informal touches (âprince charmingâ) slightly jar with academic tone
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The writing demonstrates a clear, formal register and good grammatical accuracy overall, fitting the band 4 descriptors. Minor errors in spelling, typesetting, and occasional lapses into passive or informal phrasing prevent the very high precision required for band 5.

strandSelections.reasoning: Quotations are woven into sentences with explanatory commentary the majority of the time, showing purposeful integration (ââBut I did. And do,â demonstrating the protagonistâs voice and agencyâ).  Occasional stand-alone quotes and missing page citations keep the integration from being flawless, yet overall the examples are âmostly well integratedâ, justifying band 4.

The student refers confidently to four separate stories from the collection and demonstrates a secure grasp of plot events and Carterâs overarching feminist purpose.  Connections to the historical moment of second-wave feminism and to the conventions of traditional fairy tales show an awareness of wider implications that is sustained across the essay.  Although the discussion sometimes remains general and does not probe the more complex ambiguities of the texts, overall knowledge and understanding are clearly good rather than merely satisfactory.

The essay follows a logical structure: introduction, three themed body sections, and conclusion, each signposted with headings.  Paragraphs usually develop one clear idea and link back to the central inquiry of gender-role subversion, giving the piece cohesion.  Minor repetition and the occasional disruptive heading inserted mid-paragraph prevent it from being fully âeffectively organizedâ, but it is certainly âwell organized and mostly cohesiveâ, fitting band 4.

Quotations are drawn from each story and are usually relevant to the point being made (e.g., the motherâs courage, the heroineâs laughter, the âthrusting pistonsâ metaphor).  These references generally anchor the commentary and move the argument forward.  While citations lack page numbers and some points rely on paraphrase, the frequency and aptness of textual support effectively buttress the studentâs ideas, meeting the descriptor for band 4.

The student maintains a formal analytical register, employs third-person academic diction, and uses appropriate connective phrases.  While there are sporadic conversational touches (âprince charmingâ or âmadly in loveâ), these do not dominate the overall style, which remains consistently suited to an HL literary essay.  Thus, the work fits the description of band 4 for register and style.

Language is generally clear and fluent with varied sentence structures and appropriate academic vocabulary (e.g., âemphasising the female capacity for agencyâ).  Errors such as âin turn makes it so thatâ or misspellings like âindependantâ are present but do not impede comprehension.  This level corresponds to âclear with good accuracyâ in band 4.

The essay offers clear analytical commentsâidentifying Carterâs inversion of the âdamselâ trope, the sensual imagery of âpistonsâ, and the grotesque description of the Marquisâshowing an ability to link textual features to meaning.  Nevertheless, many sections lapse into narrative summary or broad assertion (âCarter successfully subvertsâ¦â) without probing how specific stylistic choices achieve the effect, and evaluation of authorial intention is limited.  This aligns with âgenerally appropriate analysisâ in band 3.

annotations.comment: The introduction effectively situates Carterâs collection within second-wave feminism, demonstrating good contextual understanding. Consider briefly foreshadowing your main analytical points to sharpen the thesis.
This paragraph relies heavily on summary rather than analysis. The student should integrate interpretation of Carterâs language here rather than retelling plot events.
The in-text citations omit page numbers, which weakens the academic rigour. Include specific page references for each quotation to strengthen textual support.
Repetitive use of âoftentimesâ and passive constructions dilute clarity. Vary adverbs and streamline sentence structures for greater precision.
Quotations from âThe Bloody Chamberâ and âThe Tigerâs Brideâ are well integrated with commentary, demonstrating purposeful example integration.
The analysis asserts that Carter âflips the narrativeâ but stops short of unpacking how syntax or imagery enact this subversion. Develop closer scrutiny of her stylistic choices.
Consider breaking this lengthy paragraph into two focused sections: one on maternal heroism and another on laughter as agency. This will improve structural clarity.
Typographical error: âCarters 'The Tiger's Bride'â should read âCarterâs âThe Tigerâs Brideâ.â Correct possessive form for accuracy.
The subheading interrupts mid-analysis and appears twice across pages, disrupting flow. Ensure headings align with paragraph breaks for cohesion.
Insightful reading of the âthrusting pistonsâ metaphor links mechanical imagery to sexual awakening, showing appropriate and sometimes insightful analysis.
The transition to âThe Company of Wolvesâ example begins abruptly without a topic sentence. Introduce each new story with a clear analytical frame.
The story synopsis outweighs interpretation in this section. The student should balance summary with how diction and tone convey subversion.
The analysis of the protagonistâs laughter (ânobodyâs meatâ) convincingly links humor to agencyâthis is a strong evaluative observation.
The phrase âBy introducing this plot pointâ¦â is a broad evaluative claim. Specify which narrative technique (e.g., irony, pacing) Carter uses to achieve this effect.
Strong contrast between the waxen Marquis and the heroic mother highlights emasculation effectively, showing nuanced understanding of Carterâs choice.
The transition to âPuss-in-Bootsâ lacks a clear connective sentence. Signal this shift more explicitly to maintain cohesion.
Overreliance on summary of âPuss-in-Bootsâ outcome reduces analytical depth. Shift focus to Carterâs ironic tone and its feminist implications.
Clear hierarchical organization (intro, bodies, conclusion) shows strong structural control. Paragraph sequencing generally enhances the line of inquiry.
Conclusion restates prior points rather than offering a final synthesis. Consider drawing a broader implication or critical insight to conclude powerfully.
Minor spelling error: âindependantâ should be âindependent.â Proofread for such lapses to maintain academic tone.
Bibliography shows awareness of secondary sources but entries lack consistency in formatting. Adopt a single citation style rigorously.
The stray LaTeX fragment (â\qquad \qquad -â) appears to be an artifact. Remove or replace with proper bibliographic content.
Consider integrating page numbers into your text to strengthen credibility: e.g., (Carter, 1979, p. 23) for direct quotes.
Bibliography properly credits both primary and secondary materials, demonstrating good awareness of sources.
The repeated section heading at the top of page 5 duplicates page 3âs title. Ensure each header reflects the content immediately below.
Link the discussion of male diminishment more explicitly back to your central argument about feminist subversion for tighter cohesion.
